prTitle: feat: build Swift Charts components and TimeRangePicker for progress analytics
prBody:
## Summary

Implements **task-ios-fitness-tracker-app-feat-progress-analytics-2**: Build Swift Charts components and TimeRangePicker.

### Changes

- **`ProgressViewModel.swift`** — `@Observable` view model with:
  - `TimeRange` enum (1W / 1M / 3M / All) with correct `startDate` calculations
  - `WeightDataPoint` and `StrengthDataPoint` value types for Swift Charts
  - Background weight loading from `ProgressRepository` with date-range filtering
  - Per-exercise Epley 1RM aggregation from `WorkoutRepository` sessions
  - Weekly down-sampling for all-time weight data (keeps aggregation < 300 ms)
  - `selectedRange` `didSet` triggers automatic reload
  - Auto-selects first available exercise when exercises are populated

- **`TimeRangePicker.swift`** — Segmented control binding to `ProgressViewModel.selectedRange`, cycling through all four `TimeRange` cases with accessible labels

- **`WeightChartView.swift`** — Swift Charts line + area chart showing bodyweight trend:
  - `catmullRom` interpolation for smooth curves
  - Dynamic Y-axis domain with 15% padding
  - Per-range x-axis date formats (weekday / month-day / month-year)
  - Empty state via `ContentUnavailableView` when no data
  - Loading state via `ProgressView`

- **`StrengthChartView.swift`** — Swift Charts per-exercise 1RM trend chart:
  - Exercise `Menu` picker populated from `viewModel.availableExercises`
  - Same line + area chart pattern as WeightChartView
  - Shows Epley formula disclaimer
  - Empty states for no sessions and no data for selected exercise

- **`ProgressViewModelTests.swift`** — 16 unit tests covering:
  - `TimeRange.startDate` calculations (1W, 1M, 3M, all-time nil)
  - Weight metric loading, filtering, and non-weight metric exclusion
  - All-time down-sampling returns data points
  - Epley 1RM formula accuracy (`100 kg × 5 reps = 116.67 kg`)
  - Auto-selection of first exercise
  - Error message population on repository failure
  - `isLoading` lifecycle
  - `currentStrengthPoints` returns empty without selected exercise

Closes #64
